what_it_does: A JSON registry that enumerates archived HTML files and records per-file structural metadata, content counts, dependency edges, and code-style conventions.
observations: Contains 19 entries, all .html files under archive/
studio.html is a large interactive single-file app (43 KB, 650 lines, 21 JS functions); all others are smaller static mockups
Most entries describe 1600×900 fixed-viewport layouts with CSS-only styling
Edges include Google Fonts preconnect/stylesheet links, local fetches, API fetches, POST targets, and JS calls to DNA.* methods
Only studio.html and var-15-currentsolution.html contain JavaScript; var-15 has a single inline script
Several files include inline SVG diagrams (test-B, var-13, var-14, var-16)
content_counts keys vary per file, e.g. SVG_elements and JS_functions appear only where relevant
Most edges have resolved: null; only studio.html's script-src resolves to ui/surface.js
Conventions differ sharply: studio.html uses formatted 2-space CSS/JS, while var-* files use minified single-line CSS
Final newline is true only for studio.html; all others are false
File headers are absent except in studio.html and a few var-* files with CSS comment headers
standouts: studio.html is an outlier: a 3-column instrument/stage/tray app with API calls and a resolved surface.js dependency
var-14-routingtree.html has only 6 CSS rule blocks but a 56-line inline SVG routing tree
var-15-currentsolution.html is the only non-studio file with JavaScript, used to scatter clutter tiles
The test-A/B/C files use formatted CSS and no comments, while var-* files use minified CSS with inline comments
Many entries share identical Google Fonts edges, suggesting a common typographic baseline
conventions: Top-level files array with uniform object keys per entry
Filenames use kebab-case and encode intent (test-A-cool-precise, var-9-truetone)
Edges use direction 'in'/'out' with kind labels like link-preconnect, fetch, fetch-post, js-call
Unresolved targets are represented as null in resolved
Conventions object captures indentation, quote style, casing, line length, comment style, and final_newline
Single-quote strings in JS and double-quote HTML attributes where JS is present
camelCase for JS identifiers, kebab-case for CSS classes
concerns: Many fetch/API edges are unresolved, so it is unclear whether they are aspirational or actual dependencies
Inconsistent final_newline and file_header presence suggests manual curation
content_counts schema is not uniform, which may complicate automated parsing
No schema version or timestamp field
studio.html's JS is minified with no indentation, conflicting with its otherwise documented 2-space convention
Edges mix relative paths, absolute API paths, and conceptual JS targets without a clear resolution policy
notes: A hand-curated metadata ledger for archived HTML prototypes, capturing layout fingerprints, dependency edges, and code-style conventions in one queryable file.
questions: Is this file generated by a tool or maintained manually?
What does resolved: null signify versus a resolved path?
What are DNA.surfaces, DNA.injectVars, and DNA.mountAtlas intended to represent?
Why do content_counts keys differ across entries rather than using a fixed schema?
What is the meaning of the canon comment in studio.html?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as a census/registry of archive HTML files, which matches its stated purpose.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a neutral, queryable inventory of archived design/code substrates so they can be discovered, compared, or fused without imposing a single canonical winner.
novelty: The combination of structural summary, content counts, dependency edges, and per-file style conventions in one ledger is distinctive; it treats code files as design substrates to be catalogued.
contribution: Provides a normalized metadata layer over raw HTML files, capturing layout types, asset dependencies, unresolved API edges, and code-style conventions that downstream tooling can use for retrieval, comparison, or fusion.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON census registry of 19 archived HTML design prototypes with per-file structure summaries, content counts, dependency edges, and code conventions.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and characterize archived HTML mockups and app substrates for structured discovery and downstream design-system analysis.
